evals.prompt: Draw me the architecture of this application
evals.expected_output: C4 container diagram saved as .drawio file and rendered via drawio-mcp
expectations: Analyzes root configs, entry points, and top-level directories
Generates mxGraph XML with real names from code
Applies color coding per layer (data=blue, logic=green, interface=yellow, external=red)
Calls create_diagram MCP tool for preview

evals.prompt: Create an ERD for this project's database
evals.expected_output: ERD diagram with tables, columns, PK/FK markers, and relationship cardinality
expectations: Auto-detects ORM and reads model definitions
Extracts tables, columns, types, foreign keys, relationships
Includes cardinality labels on relationships (1, N, M)

evals.prompt: Show me the login flow as a sequence diagram
evals.expected_output: Sequence diagram with lifelines for each component involved in the login flow
expectations: Traces the login route handler through all called functions
Generates sequence diagram with lifelines and labeled arrows
Uses real function and file names from the code
No XML comments in generated mxGraph XML
Does not fall back to Mermaid or text diagrams

evals.prompt: Draw a diagram for an empty project with no source code
evals.expected_output: Gracefully handles missing code — informs user, does not generate empty or placeholder diagram
expectations: Does NOT generate a Mermaid (.mmd) file
Does NOT create a placeholder diagram with generic labels
Informs user that no code was found to diagram
Suggests what the user can provide for a useful diagram
